versionNotes: Sprint 2 demo slide builder — Symbiotic Thinking Dojo activity for building 5-slide growth narrative with self-evaluation
title: Demo Slide Builder: Guided Symbiotic Thinking Dojo
description:
This is a Symbiotic Thinking Dojo activity. You're developing your thinking WITH AI, not outsourcing it TO AI. Bring shallow thinking, get polished shallow output. Bring deep engagement, build a genuine growth narrative.

Your Sprint 2 demo uses 5 slides to tell a growth story: (1) The quality & why it matters, (2) Your starting point, (3) What you did, (4) Evidence of growth, (5) Your trajectory / what's next.

For each slide, enter your honest first draft. The AI will push you to be more specific, more evidence-based, and more honest. Your refined responses become the content for your actual presentation slides.

The final step is a self-evaluation: you'll rate your own slides against the demo criteria and justify each rating. The AI will challenge inflated or deflated self-assessments.

questions.prompt: Slide 1 — What specific quality or skill are you demonstrating growth in? Why does it matter — not in general, but to YOU and your career goals? Don't just name it. Name the specific gap you identified.
questions.partnerInstructions: Enter your draft for Slide 1. Be specific about the skill AND the gap you identified. Generic answers like 'communication' will get pushed back.
questions.placeholder: Name the quality, the specific gap, and why it matters to you personally...
questions.aiContext: This is a Symbiotic Thinking Dojo activity for Sprint 2 demo preparation in CST349 (Professional Seminar). The student is working individually to draft Slide 1 of a 5-slide growth narrative. They need to name a SPECIFIC quality and a SPECIFIC gap — not a generic skill. 'Communication' is too broad. 'Active listening — specifically the gap where I hear words but miss meaning' is specific. Push them on: Is this specific enough that it could be WRONG? Is the gap something they identified through evidence, or just a feeling? Why does this matter for THEIR career specifically, not careers in general?
questions.discussionPrompt: Review the AI's questions. Is your quality specific enough to be testable? Is your gap honest or sanitized? Refine your thinking below.

questions.prompt: Slide 2 — Where were you at the beginning of Sprint 2? What's your evidence for this baseline — not how you felt, but what you can show? Reference your Evidence Design from Week 5.
questions.partnerInstructions: Enter your draft for Slide 2. Include specific evidence of your starting point — artifacts, recordings, feedback — not just a self-assessment.
questions.placeholder: Describe your baseline with evidence — recordings, feedback, specific examples with dates...
questions.aiContext: This is Slide 2 of a Sprint 2 demo in CST349. The student is working individually. They should have a documented baseline from their Evidence Design (Week 5). Strong baselines have: specific artifacts (video recordings, writing samples, peer feedback), dates, and measurable aspects. Weak baselines are: 'I felt like I wasn't good at it,' or 'I knew I needed to improve.' Push them on: Where's the artifact? Would a stranger understand your starting point from this description? What does your Evidence Design say your baseline plan was? Did you actually capture it?
questions.discussionPrompt: Is your baseline documented with actual evidence or just self-reported? Would someone outside this class understand where you started? Refine below.

questions.prompt: Slide 3 — What specific actions did you take? Not 'I practiced' — what exactly, when, how often? What did your triad help with? What did you try that didn't work?
questions.partnerInstructions: Enter your draft for Slide 3. List specific actions with dates and observable outputs. Include what didn't work — a demo that only shows successes isn't honest.
questions.placeholder: List specific actions, dates, frequency, and what your triad contributed...
questions.aiContext: This is Slide 3 of a Sprint 2 demo in CST349. The student is working individually. They should list specific, verifiable actions — not intentions or general efforts. Strong entries include: dates, frequency, observable outputs, and what their triad contributed. Push them on: What's the difference between effort and progress? You said you 'practiced' — practiced what, when, how? What did you try that FAILED and what did that teach you? A demo that only shows successes isn't honest.
questions.discussionPrompt: Are these verifiable actions or just intentions? What's missing — especially things that didn't work? Refine below.

questions.prompt: Slide 4 — This is the heart of your demo. Show before/after comparisons, external feedback, concrete examples with dates. What would convince a skeptic who has never met you? If you heard something at the networking event that changed your thinking, that's evidence too.
questions.partnerInstructions: Enter your draft for Slide 4. This needs to be your strongest slide. Include before/after comparisons, external feedback, and specific examples — not just self-assessment.
questions.placeholder: Lay out your evidence — before/after, external feedback, specific examples with dates, networking event insights...
questions.aiContext: This is Slide 4 of a Sprint 2 demo in CST349 — the most important slide. The student is working individually. The key distinction: evidence of GROWTH vs. evidence of EFFORT. 'I did 5 practice sessions' = effort. 'In week 5 I interrupted 4 times in 10 minutes; in week 7 I interrupted once and referenced teammates 5 times' = growth. Push HARD on: Is this evidence of change, or just evidence you did things? Do you have before/after comparisons with actual artifacts? If they reference the networking event, ask: What specific thing did a professional say that confirmed or broke an assumption? That's strong evidence. If the student has only self-assessment and no external feedback or artifacts, flag it directly: self-assessment alone is the weakest form of evidence.
questions.discussionPrompt: Does your evidence prove growth or just effort? What's your strongest single piece of evidence? Is any of it externally validated? Refine below.

questions.prompt: Slide 5 — Where are you headed? What's the honest gap between where you are now and where you want to be? What will you continue working on? What did this sprint teach you about how YOU grow — not the skill itself, but your learning process?
questions.partnerInstructions: Enter your draft for Slide 5. Be honest about the remaining gap. The strongest trajectories include what you learned about your own learning process.
questions.placeholder: Your honest assessment: where you are now, the remaining gap, and what you learned about how you learn...
questions.aiContext: This is Slide 5 of a Sprint 2 demo in CST349 — the trajectory slide. The student is working individually. Students often default to vague optimism here: 'I'll keep working on it.' Push for specificity: What EXACTLY will you do differently? What did this sprint teach you about how you learn — not what you learned about the skill, but what you learned about your own learning process (metacognition)? The strongest trajectories are honest about the remaining gap AND specific about what comes next. Ask: If you repeated Sprint 2 with the same skill, what would you do differently from day one?
questions.discussionPrompt: Is your trajectory specific or vague optimism? What did you learn about how YOU learn, not just what you learned? Refine below.

questions.prompt:
Self-Evaluation — You've drafted all 5 slides. Now grade yourself honestly. For each criterion below, rate yourself Strong / Developing / Weak and give a one-sentence justification.

1. Specificity: Is my skill and gap specific enough to be wrong, or is it generic?
2. Baseline evidence: Do I have documented proof of where I started, or just memory?
3. Honest actions: Did I include failures and triad contributions, or just a highlight reel?
4. Growth vs. effort: Does Slide 4 prove I changed, or just that I tried?
5. External validation: Do I have evidence from outside my own head — triad feedback, professional input, artifacts?
6. Metacognition: Do I show what I learned about my LEARNING PROCESS, not just the skill?

After rating all six, answer: What is the single most impactful improvement I can make before Wednesday?
questions.partnerInstructions: Rate yourself honestly on all 6 criteria. Use Strong / Developing / Weak for each with a one-sentence justification. Then identify your single most impactful improvement.
questions.placeholder:
1. Specificity: [Strong/Developing/Weak] — ...
2. Baseline evidence: [Strong/Developing/Weak] — ...
3. Honest actions: [Strong/Developing/Weak] — ...
4. Growth vs. effort: [Strong/Developing/Weak] — ...
5. External validation: [Strong/Developing/Weak] — ...
6. Metacognition: [Strong/Developing/Weak] — ...

Most impactful improvement before Wednesday: ...
questions.aiContext: This is the self-evaluation step of a Sprint 2 Demo Slide Builder in CST349. The student has drafted all 5 slides and is now rating themselves on 6 criteria derived from the demo rubric. Your job is to challenge their self-assessment — not to be harsh, but to be calibrating. Common patterns to push on: (1) Rating everything 'Developing' to appear humble without actually differentiating. Push: which of these is genuinely your weakest? Own it. (2) Rating 'Strong' on criteria where their earlier slide drafts don't support it — if their Slide 4 had no external feedback but they rate External Validation as Strong, call it out. (3) Deflecting with vague improvement plans like 'I'll add more detail.' Push: detail about WHAT specifically? Which slide, which sentence, what evidence will you add? (4) Most students will be weak on external validation — if they acknowledge this honestly, that's good metacognition. If they avoid it, push. The goal is calibrated self-assessment: seeing yourself accurately is itself evidence of growth.
questions.discussionPrompt: Look at your ratings honestly. Do they match what you actually wrote in slides 1-5? What one concrete, specific thing will you fix before Wednesday? Refine below.
